pages.name: Home
pages.description: Entry point for the IT and media services business, routing users quickly to key services and tasks, and surfacing featured case studies and help topics.
primary_functions: Introduce the company’s core IT, hosting, media, and marketing services with clear value propositions.
Provide prominent calls-to-action to web hosting plans, video production quotes, digital marketing bundles, consultations, and support.
Offer quick access to case studies and the help center for users researching solutions.

incoming_params.description: Optional anchor key (e.g., 'web_hosting', 'video_production', 'digital_marketing', 'consultations', 'support') used to auto-scroll to a specific section on the home page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on primary CTA like 'View Web Hosting Plans' or hosting teaser card.
Click on 'Media Services' CTA in hero or services grid.
Click on 'Video Production' focused CTA such as 'Request a Video Quote'.
Click on 'Digital Marketing' or 'Build Your Bundle' CTA.
Click on 'Book a Consultation' CTA.
Click on 'View All Case Studies' from featured case studies section.
Click on 'Browse Help Center' from featured help topics.
Click on 'Get Support' or similar support CTA.
Click on 'IT Services (RMM)' CTA card in services summary.

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry via 'Home' link.
Default landing URL of the site (index.html).

pages.name: Web Hosting Plans
pages.description: Listing and comparison page for web hosting plans with filters and sorting to help users quickly identify the best-fit hosting option.
primary_functions: Display all available web hosting plans with key details such as storage, bandwidth, and monthly pricing.
Allow users to filter plans by storage (including minimum 50GB), billing cycle (e.g., monthly), and other attributes.
Provide sorting options such as price low-to-high to help users find the cheapest suitable plan.
Enable users to click through to a specific hosting plan’s detail page via 'View Details' or 'Select Plan'.

incoming_params.description: Optional minimum storage filter (in GB) applied on initial load; used to pre-filter for requirements like at least 50GB.
Optional billing cycle to pre-select plan pricing view; allowed values: 'monthly' or 'annual'.
Optional initial sort key such as 'price_low_to_high' or 'featured'.
Optional maximum monthly price to apply as an initial filter (e.g., 40 for plans under $40/month).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'View Details' or 'Select Plan' on any hosting plan card.
Click on site logo or 'Back to Home' control from this page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Web Hosting' header navigation link.
Linked from home page hosting CTAs and other marketing content.
Filters and sorting can be deep-linked using query parameters such as storage_gb_min, billing_cycle, sort_by, and price_max.

pages.name: Hosting Plan Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a specific web hosting plan, with configuration and purchase options leading into the cart flow.
primary_functions: Show full specifications of the selected hosting plan, including storage, features, and monthly price.
Allow users to select required configuration options (e.g., number of websites, support tier) with default or lowest-cost settings preselected.
Provide an 'Add to Cart' or 'Continue' button to place the hosting plan into the shopping cart.
Offer a clear path to proceed toward checkout after adding the plan to the cart.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the hosting plan whose details and configuration options should be displayed.
Optional override of the displayed billing cycle for this plan, e.g., 'monthly' or 'annual'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Add to Cart' or 'Continue' after plan configuration; navigates to cart to review the selected hosting plan.
Click on 'Back to All Hosting Plans' to return to the listing with previous filters restored.

access_methods.description: Accessed when a hosting_plan_id query parameter is present, typically from the hosting plans listing.
May be linked directly from promotional content, emails, or the home page spotlighting a specific plan.

pages.name: RMM Plans
pages.description: Plan listing page for Remote Monitoring & Management (RMM) services, allowing comparison by device counts and pricing.
primary_functions: Display available RMM plans with key information such as supported device counts and monthly pricing.
Allow users to filter plans by minimum number of devices (e.g., at least 25 devices).
Provide sorting options such as price low-to-high to identify the most affordable qualifying plan.
Enable navigation to individual RMM plan detail pages via 'View Plan' or 'Details' actions.

incoming_params.description: Optional service key (e.g., 'remote_monitoring_management') used to confirm context when arriving from the 'IT Services (RMM)' navigation link.
Optional minimum number of devices used to pre-filter plans on initial load (e.g., 25 for at least 25 devices).
Optional sort key such as 'price_low_to_high' or 'min_devices_low_to_high'.
Optional maximum monthly price used to pre-filter RMM plans.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'View Plan' or 'Details' for any RMM plan.
Click on a 'Back to Home' or 'Explore All Services' control.

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'IT Services (RMM)' header link, which includes serviceId=remote_monitoring_management.
Linked from home page IT services section or other marketing content.
Deep links can specify min_devices, sort_by, and price_max query parameters.

pages.name: RMM Plan Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a specific RMM plan, including features, device limits, pricing, and the free trial signup flow.
primary_functions: Present detailed features, device limits, and pricing for a selected RMM plan.
Provide a 'Start Free Trial' or 'Try It Free' button that exposes the trial signup form for this plan.
Capture trial signup information such as full name, company name, business email, and estimated number of devices.
Submit the trial request and confirm that the free trial account has been initiated.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the RMM plan whose details and free trial options should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Back to All RMM Plans' link.
Click on 'Discuss RMM Strategy' CTA after viewing plan details or trial confirmation.

access_methods.description: Accessed with rmm_plan_id as a query parameter from the RMM plans listing or direct links.

pages.name: Media Services & Video Production
pages.description: Overview of media services with a focused section for video production and an embedded quote request flow for corporate and promotional video projects.
primary_functions: Describe the company’s media offerings, including video production, editing, and related services.
Provide a dedicated Video Production section that outlines use cases like corporate promo videos and available quality levels (e.g., 4K).
Offer a 'Request a Quote' or 'Get a Quote' button that scrolls to or reveals a video production quote form on the same page.

Allow users to submit a video production quote request by entering project description (e.g., '90-second corporate promo video'), location, budget (e.g., up to $8,000), quality preferences, and contact details.

incoming_params.description: Optional service key such as 'video_production'; when present, page auto-scrolls to the Video Production section and exposes the quote form.
Optional project title or description used to prefill the video quote form title field.
Optional maximum budget in USD to prefill the budget field in the video quote form.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'View Video Production Case Studies' link within the media services overview.
Click on 'Book a Media Strategy Consultation' CTA.

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Media Services' and 'Video Production' header links (the latter passes serviceId=video_production).
Linked from home page media/video CTAs and campaign landing content.
Query parameters like serviceId, prefill_title, and prefill_budget can tailor the initial state of the page and quote form.

pages.name: Marketing Services & Bundle Builder
pages.description: Digital marketing overview page that includes a configurable bundle builder for SEO, social media, and blog content under a target monthly budget.
primary_functions: Introduce the company’s digital marketing services, including SEO, social media management, and blog/content creation.
Provide an interactive 'Build Your Bundle' section where users can select an SEO plan (up to a price limit), a social media plan, and a blog content plan.
Display a running total monthly price for the selected bundle and help users keep the bundle at or below a chosen budget (e.g., $900/month).
Enable users to submit a 'Request Proposal' or 'Get This Plan' form with their chosen bundle configuration and contact information.

incoming_params.description: Optional numeric budget (e.g., 900) to prefill the bundle builder’s monthly budget constraint.
Optional SEO plan tier to pre-select in the bundle builder, such as 'standard', 'premium', or 'advanced'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Talk to a Marketing Strategist' CTA from the marketing overview or after configuring a bundle.
Click on 'See Marketing Success Stories' link.

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Marketing Services' header navigation link.
Linked from the home page and other pages promoting digital marketing bundles.
Budget and default plan tier can be configured via query parameters such as budget_limit and prefill_seo_tier.

pages.name: Consultations Booking
pages.description: Online scheduling page for booking consultations such as website redesign sessions, with topic selection, online format, and a time-slot calendar.
primary_functions: Allow users to choose a consultation topic (e.g., 'Website Redesign') and a meeting format (e.g., 'Online' or 'Virtual').
Provide a calendar and date picker to move to future weeks, including the next week starting Monday, and view available 30-minute time slots.
Enable selection of the earliest available time slot within a specific time range (e.g., 2:00–5:00 PM next week).
Collect booking details such as company size, name, and email, and confirm the consultation appointment.

incoming_params.description: Optional consultation topic key such as 'website_redesign', 'it_infrastructure', or 'digital_marketing' to preselect in the booking form.
Optional meeting format key such as 'online', 'in_person', or 'phone' to preselect in the booking form.
Optional ISO date string used to set the initial week shown in the date picker when loading availability.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Back to Home' after confirming a booking.

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Consultations' header navigation link.
Linked from home page CTAs and service pages encouraging users to book time with experts.
Topic, meeting_format, and start_date can be passed in query parameters to preconfigure the booking form.

pages.name: Case Studies
pages.description: Filterable library of case studies and success stories across industries and services for prospects to explore relevant examples.
primary_functions: Display a list of case studies with titles, industries, key services used, and summaries.
Provide filters by industry (including 'Retail') and by services/solutions (including 'IT Support' and 'Video Production'), supporting multi-select.
Allow users to narrow results to case studies that match both an industry and multiple selected services.
Enable navigation to individual case study detail pages by clicking on a case study’s title or image.

incoming_params.description: Optional industry filter key such as 'retail', 'healthcare', or 'finance' applied on initial load.
Optional comma-separated list of service keys (e.g., 'it_support,video_production') used to pre-filter case studies.
Optional sort key for case studies, such as 'newest' or 'featured'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on the title or image of a case study card.
Click on 'Talk to Us About a Similar Project' CTA from the listing page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Case Studies' header navigation link.
Accessible via 'Case Studies' footer link.
Linked from featured case studies on the home page.
Industry and services filters can be pre-set using query parameters such as industry=retail&services=it_support,video_production.

pages.name: Case Study Detail
pages.description: Detailed view of a specific case study, including challenge, solution, and outcomes, with a direct path to contact sales about similar work.
primary_functions: Present an in-depth narrative of the client’s challenge, the IT and media services provided, and the outcomes achieved.
Highlight associated industries and services (e.g., Retail, IT Support, Video Production) for context.
Provide a prominent 'Contact Sales', 'Talk to Us', or 'Request a Similar Project' button.
Display an embedded contact or inquiry form where users can describe their interest, enter a short message, and provide name and email before submitting.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the case study whose full details should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Schedule a Consultation About a Similar Project' CTA from the case study detail view.
Click on a link within the case study highlighting video production capabilities.

access_methods.description: Accessed using case_study_id query parameter, typically from the case studies listing.
Featured case studies on the home page can link directly here.

pages.name: Help Center
pages.description: Central knowledge base page for searching and browsing help articles across topics such as cloud services, hosting, and email.
primary_functions: Provide a search bar that lets users search for help topics (e.g., 'cloud backup').
Display search results with options to filter by topic or category, including 'Cloud Services'.
Allow users to sort help articles by criteria such as 'Most Recent' or 'Newest First' to locate the latest content.
List articles with links to individual help article detail pages for deeper reading.

incoming_params.description: Optional search query (e.g., 'cloud backup') to execute automatically when the help center loads.
Optional topic key such as 'cloud_services' or 'web_hosting' used to filter search results.
Optional initial sort order, e.g., 'newest_first' or 'relevance'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on any article title in the search results list.

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Help Center' header navigation link.
Accessible via 'Help Center' footer link.
Linked from support and error messages encouraging self-service troubleshooting.
Search query, topic, and sort_order can be encoded in the URL for deep-linked help searches.

pages.name: Help Article
pages.description: Detail page for a single help or knowledge base article, with reading-list functionality to save articles for later.
primary_functions: Present the full content of a help article, including title, publication date, and topic tags.
Provide a control such as 'Add to Favorites', 'Save to Reading List', or a bookmark icon to save the article.
When saving, prompt the user to name or select a reading list (e.g., entering 'Cloud Topics') and confirm the save action.
Confirm that the article has been saved to the chosen reading list for future reference.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the help article whose content should be displayed.
Optional origin key (e.g., 'search_results') used for 'Back to results' navigation.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Back to Help Center' or 'Back to Search Results' link.

access_methods.description: Accessed with help_article_id query parameter from the help center or deep links.

pages.name: Support & Ticket Submission
pages.description: Support hub for accessing assistance resources and submitting support tickets, including structured tickets for email and collaboration issues.
primary_functions: Summarize available support channels and link to self-service resources such as the Help Center.
Provide a 'Submit a Ticket' or 'Open a Support Request' button that reveals or scrolls to a ticket submission form on the same page.

Offer a structured ticket form where users select customer type (e.g., 'Existing Customer'), issue category (e.g., 'Email & Collaboration'), subject, detailed description, priority level, and contact email.
Submit the completed support ticket and present confirmation that the request has been received.

incoming_params.description: Optional issue category key (e.g., 'email_collaboration' or 'web_hosting') used to preselect the ticket category.
Optional priority preselection for the ticket, such as 'high' or 'urgent'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Search the Help Center' link from the support overview.

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Support' header navigation link.
Accessible via 'Support' footer link.
Linked from error messages or service issues prompting users to open support tickets.
Query parameters issue_category and priority can be used to preconfigure the ticket form.

pages.name: Cart
pages.description: Shopping cart page for reviewing selected services before checkout, including hosting plans added by the user.
primary_functions: Display all items the user has added (such as a chosen hosting plan) with names, key options, and monthly pricing.
Allow users to remove items or adjust configurable quantities if applicable.
Show a summary of charges including subtotals and estimated total monthly cost.
Provide a 'Go to Checkout' or 'Review Order' button to move to the checkout summary page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Go to Checkout' or 'Review Order' button.
Click on 'Continue Shopping – Web Hosting' link.
Click on 'Add IT Monitoring Services' link.

access_methods.description: Accessed after adding a hosting plan from the plan detail page, or via a cart icon in the global header.

pages.name: Checkout
pages.description: Order review and checkout summary page for finalizing service purchases based on the current cart contents.
primary_functions: Present a detailed order summary listing all items in the cart, including the selected hosting plan and its monthly price.
Display billing frequency and total expected recurring charges for the order.
Collect essential billing and contact information such as name, company, and email to complete the purchase.
Offer a final confirmation or 'Place Order' action (even if the primary task is only to reach this summary page).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Back to Cart' to modify items before placing the order.
Click on 'Return to Home' or 'Need Help?' after order placement or if the user cancels.

access_methods.description: Accessed from the cart page via 'Go to Checkout' button.

pages.name: About
pages.description: Background information about the IT and media services company, including mission, history, and areas of expertise.
primary_functions: Explain the company’s history, mission, and expertise in IT, hosting, media, and marketing services.
Highlight team experience, certifications, and client logos to build trust with potential customers.
Provide high-level contact and location information for users who want to learn more about the organization.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Book a Consultation' from the About page.
Click on 'View Our Work' or similar link.

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'About' footer link.
Linked from home page 'Learn more about us' section.

pages.name: Privacy Policy
pages.description: Legal page outlining how user data and privacy are handled, powered by the generic legal document retrieval interface.
primary_functions: Inform users about what personal and usage data is collected through the website and services.
Explain how data is stored, processed, and shared, including any third-party tools or tracking.
Describe users’ rights regarding their data and how they can exercise those rights.

incoming_params.description: Optional key passed to getLegalDocument; defaults to 'privacy_policy' when not provided.

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Privacy Policy' footer link.
Linked from forms and consent prompts referring to privacy details.

pages.name: Terms of Service
pages.description: Legal terms governing the use of the website and the company’s services, retrieved via the shared legal document interface.
primary_functions: Define the conditions under which users may access and use the website and related services.
Outline limitations of liability, disclaimers, and acceptable use policies.
Clarify contractual aspects such as payment terms, renewals, and termination conditions for services.

incoming_params.description: Optional key passed to getLegalDocument; defaults to 'terms_of_service' when not provided.

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Terms of Service' footer link.
Linked from sign-up, checkout, and trial initiation flows requiring agreement to terms.

header_links.description: Homepage and main entry to all services
Overview of media and video production services
Video production details and quote request form
Digital marketing services and bundle builder
Book an online or in-person consultation
Customer success stories and case studies

footer_links.description: Get support or submit a ticket
